_comment: MASTER BLUEPRINT for the TeleTracking 2026 template. The engine is brand-agnostic; this file is what makes a TeleTracking deck look like the official 2026 PowerPoint template. Each engine layout maps to a visual recipe (theme + header treatment) drawn from the real template; themes resolve to the brand palette. Color values may be a brand-palette key (e.g. 'green', 'deepNavy') or a literal #hex. A brand with no blueprint.json falls back to the engine's neutral drawkit look (the client-agnostic default).
name: TeleTracking 2026

design._comment: DESIGN LANGUAGE — the template's elements decomposed into tokens the generative renderer draws from, so slides are template-faithful AND adapt to content. Color values are semantic theme keys (card/cardInk/ink/muted/node/title_ink/rule_color, resolved per theme) or #hex. Measured from the 2026 template; tune here, no code change. A brand without `design` uses the engine's built-in (agnostic) defaults.

image._comment: image / imagetext framing — the template uses frameless, edge-to-edge pictures (no floating rounded card). fit cover fills the box; framePt 0 = no outline; radiusPct 0 = square corners. overlay* tints a full-bleed image so overlaid title text stays legible. bannerIn/insetIn set the header-banner height and the imagetext picture-column width.

sectionheader._comment: numbered SECTION BREAK (template 'Section Header'): a left number+kicker lockup, a large title, an accent rule, a supporting line. Drawn full-bleed on the dark background; distinct from the centered `divider` title screen.

cycles._comment: CIRCULAR process (template 'Cycles'): a ring of numbered nodes on the left + a numbered legend column on the right. The ring places nodes evenly for any 3-6 steps; the faint hub can hold a center label. dotColor draws small loop markers between nodes. A cycle is ONE visualization and is NEVER paginated — a too-long sequence should use `process`/`timeline` instead. Tune ring geometry here.

native._comment: NATIVE TEMPLATE-LAYOUT rendering: when present, the engine builds on this brand's real PowerPoint template and pours content into the named layout's placeholders, so the template's actual elements/styles/image slots come through. Each engine layout lists candidate template layouts; the engine picks by item `count` (a candidate with no `count` matches any). If no candidate fits, the engine falls back to its generative renderer on the brand background. `title`/`intro`/`subtitle` are placeholder idxs; `slots` are the repeating content holders in visual order; `kind` says how to fill them.
